title: phyla linearis
intro: Phyla linearis, also known as frog's breath or Texas frog's breath, is a flowering plant in the mint family that is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, antiviral, and antibacterial properties, which can help to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory infections and skin conditions. The medicinal properties of Phyla linearis are attributed to its essential oils, which contain compounds such as limonene and beta-pinene, and can be used to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and promote wound healing. Phyla linearis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
